data.content: Found the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn doesn't know about it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild `CoreCompile` target uses the `@(Compile)` items that were already collected before this target runs.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, so Roslyn picks it up:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target, after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild collects compile items before `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file was simply ignored by the compiler.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
